name: Naamkaran
description: Naamkaran is the sacred Hindu naming ceremony performed for a newborn. It is a joyous occasion where the child's name is chosen based on astrology and family tradition.
requirements: Child's horoscope, family members, offerings (flowers, coconut, sweets)
benefits: Blessings for the child, positive identity, alignment with planetary energies

name: Griha Pravesh
description: Griha Pravesh is the ritual performed before entering a new home. It purifies the space and invites positive energies into the house.
requirements: Kalash, coconut, turmeric, rice, priest, family presence
benefits: Removes negative energy, brings peace and prosperity to the home

name: Antyeshti (Last Rites)
description: Antyeshti is the final rite in the life cycle, performed to ensure the departed soul attains moksha. It includes cremation and rituals for peace of the soul.
requirements: Funeral arrangements, priest, family, offerings, ash immersion items
benefits: Spiritual liberation of the soul, emotional closure for the family

name: Satyanarayan Katha
description: This katha is performed to express gratitude to Lord Vishnu and seek his blessings. It is often conducted during housewarmings, weddings, or festivals.
requirements: Idol/photo of Lord Vishnu, prasad, priest, tulsi leaves, banana leaves
benefits: Brings prosperity, removes obstacles, ensures success in ventures

name: Upanayanam (Sacred Thread Ceremony)
description: Upanayanam is the sacred thread ceremony signifying the start of formal education and spiritual study for young boys.
requirements: Yajnopavita (thread), family, priest, fire offerings, new clothes
benefits: Spiritual discipline, blessings for education and knowledge

name: Shraddha (Ancestral Ritual)
description: Shraddha is performed to honor ancestors and ensure their peace in the afterlife. It is performed on death anniversaries or during Pitru Paksha.
requirements: Cooked food, sesame seeds, water, darbha grass, photos of ancestors
benefits: Pleasing the ancestors, removal of Pitru Dosha, blessings for the family

name: Mundan (First Hair Cut)
description: Mundan is the child's first haircut ceremony. It is believed to cleanse the child of past life negativity and promote healthy growth.
requirements: Child, barber, holy water, family, offerings
benefits: Improves hair growth, purifies the body and soul, receives blessings

name: Sundarkand Path
description: Sundarkand Path is a recitation from the Ramayana that glorifies Lord Hanuman. It is recited to overcome challenges and seek divine protection.
requirements: Hanuman idol/picture, path book, diya, incense, sweets
benefits: Removes fear and negativity, brings courage and clarity

name: Navagraha Shanti
description: This puja is done to appease the nine planets and balance their influences in one's horoscope. It's commonly performed during major life changes or planetary doshas.
requirements: Navagraha yantra, specific offerings, priest, horoscope copy
benefits: Relief from planetary afflictions, peace, and progress in life

name: Havan (Homam)
description: Havan is a fire ritual where sacred offerings are made into the fire while chanting mantras. It is a purifying and auspicious ceremony for many occasions.
requirements: Havan kund, ghee, wood, samagri, priest
benefits: Cleansing of space, spiritual growth, attracting positive energy

name: Navagraha Shanti
description: A puja performed to reduce the malefic effects of planets and ensure planetary harmony.

name: Lakshmi Puja
description: Dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, this puja is performed for wealth, prosperity, and success.

name: Durga Saptashati Paath
description: A powerful recitation of 700 verses invoking Goddess Durga’s protection, power, and blessings.

name: Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran
description: A remedy ritual performed to nullify the negative effects of Kaal Sarp Yoga in one’s horoscope.

name: Pitru Paksha Shraddha
description: Offerings made to ancestors during the Pitru Paksha period to ensure peace and blessings from the departed souls.

name: Chandi Paath
description: A sacred ritual involving recitation of Devi Mahatmyam to attain protection, strength, and spiritual upliftment.

name: Rudrabhishek Puja
description: A ritual of pouring sacred items on Shiva Linga to seek Lord Shiva’s blessings.

name: Mahamrityunjaya Jaap
description: A life-saving ritual dedicated to Lord Shiva for healing and longevity.

name: Sundarkand Paath
description: Recitation of Sundarkand to overcome troubles and invoke Lord Hanuman’s blessings.

name: Satyanarayan Katha
description: A popular Hindu ritual worshipping Lord Vishnu to bring harmony and fulfillment.
